title: User Management — Toggle Account Access with Confirmation
content:
Feature: Admin — User Management — Toggle Account Access with Confirmation

Page URL: https://qa-atlas.qtsolvdev.com/admin (User Management tab)

Login Flow:
Navigate to https://qa-atlas.qtsolvdev.com/admin
Click "Salesforce Login" (or your SSO provider button)

Acceptance Criteria:

AC1. The User Management table contains an "Account Access" column.
Each user row displays a toggle switch representing the user's portal access state:
- Teal/ON = Access enabled
- Grey/OFF = Access revoked

AC2. Clicking the Account Access toggle does not immediately change its state.
Instead, a confirmation modal is displayed before any update occurs.

AC3. The confirmation modal contains:
- Circular info/warning icon centered at the top
- Title: "Change User Account Access?"
- Message:
"This action will immediately update the user's access permissions
and portal availability. Please confirm before proceeding."
- "Cancel" button (outlined style)
- "Confirm" button (filled dark-teal style)
- Close ("X") icon in the modal header

AC4. Clicking "Confirm":
- Closes the modal
- Updates the Account Access toggle state immediately
- Persists the change in the backend
- If toggle was ON, it becomes OFF
- If toggle was OFF, it becomes ON

AC5. Clicking "Cancel" or the "X" icon:
- Closes the modal
- Leaves the toggle unchanged
- Does not trigger any API request
- Does not update backend data

AC6. When Account Access is changed from ON to OFF:
- User access is revoked immediately
- Backend terminates all active sessions for that user within 60 seconds
- Any subsequent portal request from that user returns
401 Unauthorized / Access Denied

AC7. When Account Access is changed from OFF to ON:
- User access is restored immediately
- User can log in and access the portal again based on assigned permissions

AC8. The currently logged-in Portal Administrator's own Account Access toggle:
- Is visible in the table
- Is permanently disabled
- Cannot be clicked or changed
- Hovering over the disabled toggle displays:
"You cannot revoke your own access."

AC9. If the backend update fails:
- The toggle remains in its original state
- An error message is displayed to the administrator
- No partial state change is shown in the UI

User Flows:

Flow 1 — Revoke User Access:
1. Admin navigates to User Management
2. Admin clicks a teal (ON) Account Access toggle
3. Confirmation modal appears
4. Admin clicks "Confirm"
5. Modal closes
6. Toggle changes to grey (OFF)
7. Access revocation is saved in backend
8. User sessions are terminated within 60 seconds

Flow 2 — Restore User Access:
1. Admin clicks a grey (OFF) Account Access toggle
2. Confirmation modal appears
3. Admin clicks "Confirm"
4. Modal closes
5. Toggle changes to teal (ON)
6. Access restoration is saved in backend

Flow 3 — Cancel Access Change:
1. Admin clicks any Account Access toggle
2. Confirmation modal appears
3. Admin clicks "Cancel" or "X"
4. Modal closes
5. Toggle remains unchanged
6. No backend request is made

Flow 4 — Attempt to Disable Own Access:
1. Admin locates their own user row
2. Admin attempts to click the Account Access toggle
3. No action occurs
4. Toggle remains disabled
5. Tooltip displays:
"You cannot revoke your own access."

Edge Cases:

* User has active sessions on multiple devices:
  all active sessions are terminated within 60 seconds after access revocation
* User attempts to use an existing session after revocation:
  receives 401 Unauthorized / Access Denied response
* User access restoration does not automatically restore terminated sessions;
  user must authenticate again
* Network/API failure during update:
  toggle remains unchanged and an error message is shown
* Rapid repeated clicks on a toggle:
  only one confirmation modal may be displayed at a time
* Admin's own toggle remains disabled regardless of current access state
